answer:
**The Vīramitrodaya (also known as the *Samayaprakāśa* of the *Vīramitrodaya*), Chapter 1.**

This colophon appears repeatedly at the end of subsections within Chapter 1 (e.g., after the general determination of the *tithi*, after Janmāṣṭamī, etc.). One full instance is:

> Thus ends the general determination of the *tithi* in the *Samayaprakāśa* of the *Vīramitrodaya*, composed by the glorious Mitra Miśra—son of the glorious Paraśurāma Miśra, who was the son of the glorious scholar Haṃsa; who was prompted by the glorious Vīrasiṃhadeva, the sun that causes the lotus of the earth, which is the circle of the four oceans, to bloom, the son of the glorious Mahārājādhirāja Madhukarasāha, who was the son of the glorious Mahārājādhirāja Pratāparudra, whose lotus-feet were irradiated by the cluster of rays from the crest-jewels of the entire circle of vassal kings; who was proficient in all branches of knowledge, a lion to the great elephant of the world's poverty, and the life-giving herb for the learned.[^1]

[^1]: *Vīramitrodaya*, [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-viramitrodaya-d4b632/01/translation/#p-e1f944).
